items.description: Upper cluster of large calligraphic majuscules featuring prominent 'S'/'G' forms with long curving strokes and extra pen marks.
items.scholarly_note: Probatio pennae; scribe testing nib width, ink flow, and letter forms in the wide margin.

items.description: Mid-left sequence of large bold letters including forms resembling 'S G k o a n' and 'B G k o a n'.
items.scholarly_note: Pen trials exercising majuscule shapes and pen pressure; typical in Icelandic Gothic manuscripts.

items.description: Lower left cluster with large open-bowl 'A' form and 'S G v t a l r' letter sequence.
items.scholarly_note: Additional probationes pennae showing varied letter constructions and stroke order.

items.description: Long thin vertical inked line running down the outer left margin alongside the text block.
items.scholarly_note: Possibly a ruling guide, vertical boundary mark, or extended stroke from adjacent pen trials.

items.description: Large decorated initial capital with long vertical stem descending several lines and small loop/eye ornament.
items.scholarly_note: Standard scribal hierarchy marker signaling the opening of a new section or poem.

items.description: Marginal note 'hier vantar við geilrade' with a cross or star-like mark (✠).
items.scholarly_note: Contemporary annotation recording a textual lacuna ('hier vantar' = 'here is lacking/missing'). 'geilrade' refers to the specific missing passage or figure.

items.description: Note 'Bryn hilldar' written in a slightly freer ductus immediately below the lacuna annotation.
items.scholarly_note: Explicit identification of the missing content as relating to Brynhildr (Brynhildar). One of the most important marginalia in the entire codex, documenting awareness of the great lacuna.

items.description: Small asterisk- or cross-like decorative mark placed beside the lacuna note.
items.scholarly_note: Emphasizes or punctuates the lacuna annotation.

items.description: Small modern inscription 'Z a-13' (or similar) in the bottom-left corner.
items.scholarly_note: Post-medieval cataloguer's or librarian's mark, possibly a quire, shelf, or collation reference.

scratches: Long vertical inked line in left margin (M-073-04; may be ruled or incidental).
Faint hairline surface marks consistent with natural vellum grain and light handling.
damage_notes: Minor edge wear and corner rounding, most visible at bottom right and top.
Slight age-related browning and discoloration at outer margins and extremities.
Natural cockling and minor undulations of the calf vellum; no significant holes, tears, losses, or repairs.
No visible offset ink, ghosting, or transfer from the facing page.
summary: Page 73 is a clean, professionally executed page of Gothic book hand. The primary non-text features are multiple clusters of large majuscule pen trials in the wide left margin, a standard large decorated initial at the head of the text block, and a highly significant contemporary lacuna note at the foot ('hier vantar við geilrade' with 'Bryn hilldar' identification below). A modern catalog mark ('Z a-13') appears in the lower left. The vellum surface shows only the expected minor age-related wear; there are no figurative doodles, faces, animals, major stains, holes, offset ink, or obvious scribal corrections within the written area.
